name: Ducasse d'Ath (Giants of Ath)
description: The Ducasse d'Ath is a major processional festival featuring giant figures (Goliath, David, Samson, Ambiorix, etc.) parading through the city streets. Inscribed on UNESCO ICH in 2005.

name: Ducasse de Mons / Doudou (Lumeçon dragon fight)
description: The Doudou of Mons features the Lumeçon dragon fight on Trinity Sunday, where St. George battles the dragon (Lumeçon) at the Grand-Place. Inscribed on UNESCO ICH.

name: Ommegang van Dendermonde
description: The Ommegang of Dendermonde is a decennial procession featuring giant figures including the famous Ros Beiaard (Bayard horse) with the four Aymon children. Inscribed on UNESCO ICH.

name: Ommegang van Mechelen
description: The Mechelen Ommegang is a processional festival featuring the giant figure of Op-Signoorke. Inscribed on UNESCO ICH.

name: Meyboom and Ommegang (Brussels)
description: Brussels features the Meyboom tree planting on August 9 and the Ommegang procession on the Grand-Place. Both involve processional giants. Inscribed on UNESCO ICH.

name: Gayant de Douai (Giants of Douai)
description: The Gayant festival features a family of giant figures (Gayant, his wife Marie Cagenon, and children) parading through Douai. One of the oldest giant processions in France. Inscribed on UNESCO ICH.

name: Carnival of Cassel (Giants of Cassel)
description: The Cassel carnival features the giant Reuze-Papa and Reuze-Maman figures. Inscribed on UNESCO ICH.

name: Fête de la Tarasque (Tarascon)
description: The Tarasque festival features a mythical dragon (the Tarasque) paraded through the streets of Tarascon. The tradition dates to medieval times and involves the taming of the beast by St. Martha. Inscribed on UNESCO ICH.

name: Poulain de Pézenas (Colt of Pézenas)
description: The Poulain de Pézenas is a large processional figure of a colt, paraded during Mardi Gras and other festivals. It represents the historical ties of Pézenas to the Princes of Conti. Inscribed on UNESCO ICH.
